pr.title: R-205: close plan state-machine dead-end + AI hallucination guardrails
pr.body: ## Background A user session got stuck in this exact loop: 1. AI created plan v2 as a draft. 2. AI called `plansync_plan_propose` **without `reviewers`** → plan moved to `proposed` with **zero PlanReview rows**. 3. AI called `plansync_plan_activate` → API returned 409 *"Owner must pass `?force=true` to override"* (R-055 review-gate). 4. The MCP `plansync_plan_activate` tool had no `force` parameter. 5. There was no MCP path to add reviewers to an already-`proposed` plan, nor to roll back to `draft`. 6. The AI then **hallucinated** a CLI command (`./bin/plansync plan activate --force`) from the error text. `bin/plansync` is the Terminal launcher and does not parse `plan` subcommands. The user's plan was completely stuck through MCP. Two distinct defects, treated as one fix (R-205): - **State-machine trap** — `propose` accepts zero reviewers, but `activate` rejects them, and there was no MCP escape hatch. - **AI hallucination surface** — error message and tool docs left enough ambiguity for the model to invent a fictional CLI flag. ## Approach: nested defence in four layers Each layer alone closes the trap. All four together make it impossible to reproduce the original symptom — and impossible for the model to invent a fix that does not exist. ### Layer 1 — close the trap at the source (`fix(R-205): ...the source`) `POST /plans/:id/propose` now uses three-step precedence for the reviewer set: 1. Explicit `body.reviewers` 2. Else `plan.requiredReviewers` (if non-empty) 3. **Else owner-self-review fallback** — auto-add `auth.userName` as the sole reviewer The activate gate's "zero reviewers" branch becomes unreachable for new plans. The fallback flag is recorded in `plan_proposed` activity metadata (`ownerSelfReviewFallback`) for audit. ### Layer 2 — add an MCP escape hatch for legacy data (`feat(R-205): add POST /plans/:id/withdraw...`) `POST /plans/:id/withdraw` returns a `proposed` plan to `draft` and deletes pending `PlanReview` rows. Owner-only, transactional. Even rows that pre-date the fallback (or any future bug that produces a stuck state) now have a one-call recovery path. ### Layer 3 — fix the MCP tool surface (`feat(R-205): mcp plan tools...`) - `plansync_plan_activate` gains optional `force: boolean` (URL becomes `/activate?force=true`). - New `plansync_plan_withdraw` tool mirrors the route. - `plansync_plan_propose` reviewer-arg description now spells out the auto-add-owner fallback and the immutability of the reviewer set after propose. ### Layer 4 — error-message and prompt guardrails - The 409 error from activate now explicitly says *"call the MCP tool plansync_plan_activate with `{ force: true }`, or send `POST .../activate?force=true`. There is no plansync CLI subcommand for activate."* — kills the path the model used to invent `--force`. - `AGENTS.md` and `CLAUDE.md` gain a **Plan State Machine Invariants** section pinning the four rules: reviewer precedence, the three valid exits from `proposed`, the absence of `bin/plansync plan ...` subcommands, and the fact that `force` is ignored when reviewers exist. - The `plan_activated` activity now records `forceUsed: boolean` so audit can answer "who bypassed review?". ## Test coverage - `r055-activate-zero-reviewers.test.ts` — rewritten. Legacy zero-reviewer state can only be reached via direct DB manipulation now; assertions tightened to require BOTH "plansync_plan_activate" AND "force=true" in the error message (so the AI cannot mis-read it again). - `r205-plan-state-machine.test.ts` (new) — pins propose precedence, the audit metadata for both fallback and force, and the symmetry between `forceUsed=true|false` plan_activated activities. - `r205-plan-withdraw.test.ts` (new) — happy path, withdraw → re-propose with new reviewer set (PlanReview rows must be cleared), 409 on non-`proposed` status, 403 from non-owner. - `r121-plan-tools.test.ts` — new MCP unit tests for `force` flag URL construction (with and without delegation), `force=false` leaves URL clean, and the full withdraw tool surface. - `r048-one-active-plan-per-project.test.ts` — updated to approve the auto-added owner-self review instead of relying on `?force=true`. Same R-048 concurrency invariant, exercised through the now-canonical path. ## Verification - `bash scripts/lint.sh` — clean. - `bash scripts/build.sh` — full Next.js build succeeds; `/api/projects/[projectId]/plans/[planId]/withdraw` route registered. - `vitest run` (relevant suites): 51 plan-related API tests + 29 MCP plan-tool tests + 87 shared schema tests, all passing. - Three pre-existing failures on `master` (`r143-completion-verify-observability`, `r190-github-webhook`, `tasks.test.ts F7b`, `r182-ai-calls-observability`) reproduce on master without these changes — unrelated. ## Backwards compatibility - `propose` API: callers passing explicit `reviewers` see no change. Callers omitting `reviewers` now get owner auto-added (was: zero reviewers → stuck). This is strictly safer. - `activate` API: `?force=true` semantics unchanged. Without `force`, behaviour identical for plans with reviewers; only zero-reviewer `proposed` plans see the new error wording. - Existing MCP tool calls without `force` parameter behave exactly as before. The new `force: false` default makes the URL identical to the old call. ## Commits 1. `feat(R-205): add plan_withdrawn activity & domain-event types` — schema vocabulary 2. `fix(R-205): close plan-state-machine dead-end at the source` — propose fallback, activate audit/error wording, R-055/R-048 test updates 3. `feat(R-205): add POST /plans/:id/withdraw escape hatch` — withdraw route + tests + OpenAPI 4. `feat(R-205): mcp plan tools — force flag, withdraw tool, propose docs` — MCP surface 5. `docs(R-205): plan state machine invariants in AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md` — prompt guardrails
pr.author: nanyang12138
